message:
feat(web): add zero-copy sequence transfer infrastructure for workers

Worker Types (types.ts):
- Add SequenceEncoding type: 'ascii' | 'acgt05' for canonical encodings
- Add SequenceBytesRef interface for byte-backed sequence references
  - Supports both SharedArrayBuffer (zero-copy) and transferable ArrayBuffer
  - Includes buffer, byteOffset, byteLength, length, encoding, isShared fields
- Extend SharedSequenceRef to include SequenceBytesRef fields
- Add DotPlotJob, DotPlotWorkerResponse types for postMessage workers
- Add ComparisonJob, ComparisonWorkerMessage, ComparisonDiffStats types

SharedSequencePool (SharedSequencePool.ts):
- Add getOrCreateRef(phageId, sequence) for worker-ready references
  - Returns { ref: SharedSequenceRef, transfer: Transferable[] }
  - If SAB available: zero-copy ref with empty transfer list
  - If SAB unavailable: creates transferable ArrayBuffer copy
- Add getRef(phageId) for looking up existing pool entries
- Add makeWorkerRef helper for consistent reference creation
- Add TextEncoder/Decoder fallbacks for unusual worker environments
- Use canUseSharedArrayBuffer from wasm utils instead of inline check

Index exports (index.ts):
- Export new types: SequenceEncoding, SequenceBytesRef, DotPlotJob,
  DotPlotWorkerResponse, ComparisonJob, ComparisonWorkerMessage,
  ComparisonDiffStats

This infrastructure enables efficient sequence transfer to web workers
without repeatedly structured-cloning giant strings between threads.

Refs: phage_explorer-8qk2

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

+  SequenceEncoding,

   SharedSequenceRef,
   SharedAnalysisRequest,
   SharedSearchRequest,
   SharedAnalysisWorkerAPI,
   SharedSearchWorkerAPI,
+  // postMessage worker types
+  DotPlotJob,
+  DotPlotWorkerResponse,
+  ComparisonJob,
+  ComparisonWorkerMessage,
+  ComparisonDiffStats,
 } from './types';
 
 // Re-export simulation types from core
